title: CAM Object
description: Object that represents the CAM environment of a Fusion document.

methods.name: checkAllToolpaths
methods.description: Checks if all the operations (includes those nested in sub-folders or patterns) in the document are valid and up to date.

methods.name: checkToolpath
methods.description: Checks if the operations (including those nested in sub-folders or patterns) are valid and up to date.

methods.name: checkValidity
methods.description: Checks whether the models used by the operations have changed in the mean time and invalidates the affected operations if needed. Should be used for cases where the automatic detection does not work yet, for instance when design changes are expected before a Manufacture API call. Also recommended at the beginning of a script or the beginning of an event handler.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: clearAllToolpaths
methods.description: Clears all the toolpaths in the document, including those nested in sub-folders or patterns.

methods.name: clearToolpath
methods.description: Clears all the toolpaths for the specified objects, including those nested in sub-folders or patterns.

methods.name: deleteEntities
methods.description: Deletes the specified set of entities that are associated with this product.

methods.name: export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup
methods.description: **RETIRED** This method is only valid for an additive setup and exports the default additive setup's models as a 3MF file. The 3MF also contains machine and support information.

methods.name: findAttributes
methods.description: Find attributes attached to objects in this product that match the group and or attribute name. This does not find attributes attached directly to the Product or Document objects but finds the attributes attached to entities within the product.   The search string for both the groupName and attributeName arguments can be either an absolute name value, or a regular expression. With an absolute name, the search string must match the entire groupName or attributeName, including case. An empty string will match everything. For example if you have an attribute group named "MyStuff" that contains the attribute "Length1", using the search string "MyStuff" as the group name and "Length1" as the attribute name will find the attributes with those names. Searching for "MyStuff" as the group name and "" as the attribute name will find all attributes that have "MyStuff" as the group name.   Regular expressions provide a more flexible way of searching. To use a regular expression, prefix the input string for the groupName or attributeName arguments with "re:". The regular expression much match the entire group or attribute name. For example if you have a group that contains attributes named "Length1", "Length2", "Width1", and "Width2" and want to find any of the length attributes you can use a regular expression using the string "re:Length.*". For more information on attributes see the Attributes topic in the user manual.

methods.name: generateAllSetupSheets
methods.description: Generates all of the setup sheets for all of the operations in the document

methods.name: generateAllToolpaths
methods.description: Generates or regenerates all the operations in the document, including those nested in sub-folders or patterns.

methods.name: generateSetupSheet
methods.description: Generate the setup sheets for the specified objects

methods.name: generateTemplateXML
methods.description: Generates a template for the specified Operations, Setups, or Folders and returns the template as an XML string.

methods.name: generateToolpath
methods.description: Generates or regenerates all the specified objects, including those nested in sub-folders or patterns.

methods.name: getMachiningTime
methods.description: Get the machining time for the specified objects.

methods.name: postProcess
methods.description: **RETIRED** Post all of the toolpaths (including those nested in sub-folders or patterns) for the specified objects. If post processing fails, an error message can be retrieved from the error log explaining the reason for the failure.

methods.name: postProcessAll
methods.description: **RETIRED** Post all of the toolpaths (includes those nested in sub-folders or patterns) in the document. If post processing fails, an error message can be retrieved from the error log explaining the reason for the failure.

properties.name: allMachines
properties.description: Gets an array containing all the machines in the document.

properties.name: allOperations
properties.description: Gets a collection containing all of the operations in the document. This includes all operations nested in folders and patterns.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this product.

properties.name: customGraphicsGroups
properties.description: Returns the customGraphicsGroups object associated with the CAM product.

properties.name: designRootOccurrence
properties.description: The CAM product has its own root component which has a single occurrence that references the Design root occurrence. This property returns this occurrence.

properties.name: documentStockMaterialLibrary
properties.description: Gets the document StockMaterialLibrary. The StockMaterialLibrary contains all stock materials used by any setup inside the document. Changes to the original StockMaterialLibrary will not affect any setup because the document StockMaterialLibrary is an independent copy. You can only get a valid DocumentStockMaterialLibrary when you have access to Stock Materials private preview feature and enable the feature flag.

properties.name: documentToolLibrary
properties.description: Gets the document ToolLibrary. The ToolLibrary contains all tools used by any operation inside the document. Changes to the original ToolLibrary will not affect any operation because the document ToolLibrary is an independent copy.

properties.name: exportManager
properties.description: Returns the Export Manager which provides access to the functionality to export in various formats.

properties.name: genericPostFolder
properties.description: Gets the installed post folder.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: manufacturingModels
properties.description: Returns the collection of manufacturing models in the document.

properties.name: namedViews
properties.description: Returns the NamedViews object associated with this product. The NamedViews collection provides access to the named views defined in this product and supports the creation of new named views.

properties.name: ncPrograms
properties.description: Returns the collection of NC programs in the document.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentDocument
properties.description: Returns the parent Document object.

properties.name: personalPostFolder
properties.description: Gets the personal post folder.

properties.name: productType
properties.description: Returns the product type name of this product. A list of all of the possible product types can be obtained by using the Application.supportedProductTypes property.

properties.name: selectionSets
properties.description: Returns the SelectionSets object associated with this product. If the product does not support selection sets, null is returned. The SelectionSets object is used to create and access existing selection sets.

properties.name: setups
properties.description: Returns the Setups collection that provides access to existing setups.

properties.name: temporaryFolder
properties.description: Gets the folder for temporary files.

properties.name: unitsManager
properties.description: Returns the UnitsManager object associated with this product.

properties.name: workspaces
properties.description: Returns the workspaces associated with this product.

events.name: setupActivated
events.description: The SetupActivated event fires when a setup is activated.

events.name: setupActivating
events.description: The SetupActivating event fires before a setup is activated.

events.name: setupChanged
events.description: The SetupChanged event fires when a setup is modified.

events.name: setupCreated
events.description: The SetupCreated event fires when a new setup is created.

events.name: setupDeactivated
events.description: The SetupDeactivated event fires when a setup is deactivated.

events.name: setupDeactivating
events.description: The SetupDeactivating event fires before a setup is deactivated.

events.name: setupDestroying
events.description: The setupDestroying event fires before a setup is destroyed.

related_samples.name: Generate Setup Sheets API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the setup sheets for an existing toolpath..

related_samples.name: Generate Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Create CAM Operation From Template API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates adding a new toolpath into the document using an existing CAM template. You can view the template here, although it will be loaded automatically. Run the sample script within any Fusion project containing at least one setup. A new facing operation will be created at the end of each setup discovered.

related_samples.name: Post Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates posting toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Turning Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Turning Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by opening a simple component which is then used to describe a basic turning workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing FFF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive FFF manufacturing setup and generate a toolpath.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an FFF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically oriented and arranged within the build area of the selected FFF machine. This script will also create support structures, if required, based on the orientation of each component. Finally, the script generates the toolpath for the active setup and lets the user choose if they wish to post process the resulting toolpath or if they want to simulate it.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing SLA API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive SLA manufacturing setup.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select a SLA 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically oriented and arranged within the build area of the selected SLA machine. This script will also create support structures, based on the orientation of each component.

The support and orientation operations are created from a template. The script further demonstrates how to wrap script code into a command such that only one undo entry is created for the entire script instead of one entry per internal action.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

checkAllToolpaths.title: CAM.checkAllToolpaths Method
checkAllToolpaths.description: Checks if all the operations (includes those nested in sub-folders or patterns) in the document are valid and up to date.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.checkAllToolpaths()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->checkAllToolpaths();

checkAllToolpaths.return_description: Returns true if the all operations are valid

checkToolpath.title: CAM.checkToolpath Method
checkToolpath.description: Checks if the operations (including those nested in sub-folders or patterns) are valid and up to date.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.checkToolpath(operations)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->checkToolpath(operations);

checkToolpath.return_description: Returns true if the operations are valid

parameters.name: operations
parameters.description: An Operation, Setup, Folder, or Pattern object. You can also use an ObjectCollection to specify multiple objects of any of the supported types.

checkValidity.description: Checks whether the models used by the operations have changed in the mean time and invalidates the affected operations if needed. Should be used for cases where the automatic detection does not work yet, for instance when design changes are expected before a Manufacture API call. Also recommended at the beginning of a script or the beginning of an event handler.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.checkValidity()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->checkValidity();

deleteEntities.title: CAM.deleteEntities Method
deleteEntities.description: Deletes the specified set of entities that are associated with this product.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.deleteEntities(entities)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->deleteEntities(entities);

deleteEntities.return_description: Returns True if any of the entities provided in the list were deleted. If entities were specified that can't be deleted or aren't owned by this product, they are ignored.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection containing the list of entities to delete.

export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup.title: CAM.export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup Method
export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. This method is only valid for an additive setup and exports the default additive setup's models as a 3MF file. The 3MF also contains machine and support information. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = cAM_var.export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup(filepath)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = cAM_var.export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup(filepath, addSimulationInfo, simPostProcess, simSplitSurrogates, simKeepThickeningStructure)

export3MFForDefaultAdditiveSetup.return_description: True on success, false on errors or wrong setup type

parameters.name: addSimulationInfo
parameters.description: This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

parameters.name: simPostProcess
parameters.description: This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

parameters.name: simSplitSurrogates
parameters.description: This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

parameters.name: simKeepThickeningStructure
parameters.description: This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

findAttributes.title: CAM.findAttributes Method
findAttributes.description: Find attributes attached to objects in this product that match the group and or attribute name. This does not find attributes attached directly to the Product or Document objects but finds the attributes attached to entities within the product. The search string for both the groupName and attributeName arguments can be either an absolute name value, or a regular expression. With an absolute name, the search string must match the entire groupName or attributeName, including case. An empty string will match everything. For example if you have an attribute group named "MyStuff" that contains the attribute "Length1", using the search string "MyStuff" as the group name and "Length1" as the attribute name will find the attributes with those names. Searching for "MyStuff" as the group name and "" as the attribute name will find all attributes that have "MyStuff" as the group name. Regular expressions provide a more flexible way of searching. To use a regular expression, prefix the input string for the groupName or attributeName arguments with "re:". The regular expression much match the entire group or attribute name. For example if you have a group that contains attributes named "Length1", "Length2", "Width1", and "Width2" and want to find any of the length attributes you can use a regular expression using the string "re:Length.*". For more information on attributes see the Attributes topic in the user manual.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.findAttributes(groupName, attributeName)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->findAttributes(groupName, attributeName);

findAttributes.return_description: An array of Attribute objects that were found. An empty array is returned if no attributes were found.

parameters.name: groupName
parameters.description: The search string for the group name. See above for more details.

parameters.name: attributeName
parameters.description: The search string for the attribute name. See above for more details.

generateAllSetupSheets.title: CAM.generateAllSetupSheets Method
generateAllSetupSheets.description: Generates all of the setup sheets for all of the operations in the document

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = cAM_var.generateAllSetupSheets(format, folder)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = cAM_var.generateAllSetupSheets(format, folder, openDocument)

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The document format for the setup sheet. Valid options are HTMLFormat and ExcelFormat. Limitation: "ExcelFormat" can be used in windows OS only.

parameters.name: folder
parameters.description: The destination folder to locate the setup sheet documents in.

parameters.name: openDocument
parameters.description: An option to allow to open the document instantly after the generation. By default, the document is opened.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the setup sheets for an existing toolpath..

parameters.name: openDocument
parameters.description: An option to allow to open the document instantly after the generation. By default, the document is opened.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

generateTemplateXML.title: CAM.generateTemplateXML Method
generateTemplateXML.description: Generates a template for the specified Operations, Setups, or Folders and returns the template as an XML string.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.generateTemplateXML(operations)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->generateTemplateXML(operations);

generateTemplateXML.return_description: Returns the template XML as a string.

parameters.name: operations
parameters.description: An Operation, Setup or Folder object from which to generate the template. You can also specify a collection of any combination of these object types.

generateToolpath.title: CAM.generateToolpath Method
generateToolpath.description: Generates or regenerates all the specified objects, including those nested in sub-folders or patterns.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.generateToolpath(operations)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->generateToolpath(operations);

generateToolpath.return_description: Return GenerateToolpathFuture that includes the status of the operation generation.

parameters.name: operations
parameters.description: An Operation, Setup, Folder, or Pattern object. You can also use an ObjectCollection to specify multiple objects of any of the supported types.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

related_samples.name: Generate Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Turning Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Turning Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by opening a simple component which is then used to describe a basic turning workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

getMachiningTime.title: CAM.getMachiningTime Method
getMachiningTime.description: Get the machining time for the specified objects.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.getMachiningTime(operations, feedScale, rapidFeed, toolChangeTime)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->getMachiningTime(operations, feedScale, rapidFeed, toolChangeTime);

getMachiningTime.return_description: Returns a MachiningTime object that has properties holding the calculation results.

parameters.name: operations
parameters.description: An Operation, Setup, Folder, or Pattern object. You can also use an ObjectCollection to specify multiple objects of any of the supported types.

parameters.name: feedScale
parameters.description: The feed scale value (%) to use.

parameters.name: rapidFeed
parameters.description: The rapid feed rate in centimeters per second.

parameters.name: toolChangeTime
parameters.description: The tool change time in seconds.

postProcess.title: CAM.postProcess Method
postProcess.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Post all of the toolpaths (including those nested in sub-folders or patterns) for the specified objects. If post processing fails, an error message can be retrieved from the error log explaining the reason for the failure.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.postProcess(operations, input)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->postProcess(operations, input);

parameters.name: operations
parameters.description: An Operation, Setup, Folder, or Pattern object. You can also use an ObjectCollection to specify multiple objects of any of the supported types.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The PostProcessInput object that defines the post options and parameters.

related_samples.name: Post Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates posting toolpaths in the active document.

postProcessAll.title: CAM.postProcessAll Method
postProcessAll.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Post all of the toolpaths (includes those nested in sub-folders or patterns) in the document. If post processing fails, an error message can be retrieved from the error log explaining the reason for the failure.

syntax.python: returnValue = cAM_var.postProcessAll(input)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = cAM_var->postProcessAll(input);

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The PostProcessInput object that defines the post options and parameters.

related_samples.name: Post Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates posting toolpaths in the active document.

setupActivated.title: CAM.setupActivated Event
setupActivated.description: The SetupActivated event fires when a setup is activated.

setupActivating.title: CAM.setupActivating Event
setupActivating.description: The SetupActivating event fires before a setup is activated.

setupChanged.title: CAM.setupChanged Event
setupChanged.description: The SetupChanged event fires when a setup is modified.

setupCreated.title: CAM.setupCreated Event
setupCreated.description: The SetupCreated event fires when a new setup is created.

setupDeactivated.title: CAM.setupDeactivated Event
setupDeactivated.description: The SetupDeactivated event fires when a setup is deactivated.

setupDeactivating.title: CAM.setupDeactivating Event
setupDeactivating.description: The SetupDeactivating event fires before a setup is deactivated.

setupDestroying.title: CAM.setupDestroying Event
setupDestroying.description: The setupDestroying event fires before a setup is destroyed.
